LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ROKA AKOR SAN FRANCISCO LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT ROKA AKOR SAN FRANCISCO LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PLAINTIFF NORMA SANCHEZ-ZAPATA'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ARE DENIED IN THEIR ENTIRETY. AS TO EACH CAUSE OF ACTION, THERE IS TRIABLE DISPUTE WHETHER ROKA AKOR'S ALLEGED LEGITIMATE BUSINESS REASONS FOR THE ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTIONS WERE PRETEXTUAL. THE TEMPORAL PROXIMITY OF THE ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTIONS AND THE ARGUABLE CONTRADICTIONS IN ROKA AKOR'S POSITIONS CREATE TRIABLE ISSUES. THE FOURTH ISSUE SOUGHT TO BE SUMMARILY ADJUDICATED (WHETHER MS. SANCHEZ-ZAPATA CAN RECOVER ECONOMIC DAMAGES) DOES NOT COMPLETELY DISPOSE OF AN ENTIRE CAUSE OF ACTION AND THUS CANNOT BE SUMMARILY ADJUDICATED PER CCP SECTION 437C(F)(1). THE ONLY DAMAGES ISSUE THAT CAN BE SUMMARILY ADJUDICATED IS A REQUEST FOR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

Off Calendar Jan-15-2019 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Patrick Hormillosa, Esq. 415-362-2529 of Lepera Associates for Plaintiff.
Susan Zeme, Esq. 510-652-6895 of Employment Law for Employers for Defendant ROKA AKOR SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares it's views. The Court takes a break to hold ex partes.

11:26 am Matter is recalled. Defendant Argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ROKA AKOR SAN FRANCISCO LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Roka Akor San Francisco LLC's motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication as to plaintiff Norma Sanchez-Zapata's first amended complaint are denied in their entirety. As to each cause of action, there is triable dispute whether Roka Akor's alleged legitimate business reasons for the adverse employment actions were pretextual. The temporal proximity of the adverse employment actions and the arguable contradictions in Roka Akor's positions create triable issues. The fourth issue sought to be summarily adjudicated (whether Ms. Sanchez-Zapata can recover economic damages) does not completely dispose of an entire cause of action and thus cannot be summarily adjudicated per CCP section 437c(f)(1). The only damages issue that can be summarily adjudicated is a request for exemplary damages.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
